howItWorks.tagline: Live UK Engine Market Data for Land Rover Defender 90 D250 Owners

historyTimeline.h2: Land Rover Defender 90 D250 Engine History & Production Timeline
historyTimeline.intro: The Land Rover Defender 90 D250 launched in calendar year 2020 equipped with the DT306 (AJ20D6) 3.0-litre inline-six mild-hybrid diesel engine, marking Land Rover's first application of MHEV technology in the revived Defender lineage. This single engine family has powered the D250 variant throughout its production without fundamental architecture changes, delivering consistent 249 PS output and 570 Nm torque via common-rail injection and twin-scroll turbocharging. The Land Rover Defender 90 D250 remains in production as of 2026, confirmed by active UK dealer new-stock listings and absence of manufacturer discontinuation notices.
milestones: 2020 - DT306 (AJ20D6) introduced with 249 PS / 570 Nm, marking first MHEV diesel application in modern Defender 90 with Euro 6d-ISC-FCM compliance
2022 - Minor ECU calibration update for improved AdBlue dosing efficiency; no hardware changes affecting replacement compatibility
2024 - Continued production with unchanged DT306 specification; no power revisions or emissions standard transitions for D250 designation
Present - Land Rover Defender 90 D250 still in production with DT306 (AJ20D6) engine; no successor variant announced

commonProblems.h2: Land Rover Defender 90 D250 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Land Rover Defender 90 D250 is powered by the DT306 3.0-litre inline-six Ingenium mild-hybrid diesel engine, a unit generally regarded as more robust than the smaller 2.0-litre Ingenium variants found in earlier Defender models. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded failure-point components and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section as your decision tool to compare repair versus replacement costs and get instant quotes from certified UK specialists. The DT306 fitted to the Land Rover Defender 90 D250 has no widely reported failure patterns in UK service. Most replacement demand comes from high-mileage wear, accident damage, or neglect rather than design flaws. Every rebuilt engine supplied through our network is bench-tested and covered by a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Land Rover Defender 90 D250
finalCta.paragraph: While the DT306 3.0-litre Ingenium diesel in your Land Rover Defender 90 D250 has not shown the systematic failure patterns associated with smaller Ingenium units, any modern diesel engine can suffer from high-mileage wear, oil contamination, or accidental damage. EngineMarket connects you with certified UK Land Rover Defender 90 D250 specialists offering rebuilt DT306 units with upgraded components, nationwide delivery, vehicle collection and recovery, and 12-month warranty. items.question: How much does a Land Rover Defender 90 D250 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Land Rover Defender 90 D250 engine replacement typically costs £4,500-£9,500 depending on condition and supplier type. Rebuilt DT306 units from EngineMarket specialists offer the best balance of value, warranty coverage, and long-term reliability for UK owners.
keyPoints: Used DT306 engines range £2,800-£4,200 plus fitting at £1,200-£2,000
Reconditioned units cost £3,500-£5,800 with partial warranty coverage
Full rebuilt DT306 with upgrades and 12-month warranty: £5,200-£9,500 supplied and fitted
Main dealer replacement can exceed £12,000 for new OEM units
Compare live Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engine replacement quotes now
items.cta: -> Compare live Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engine replacement quotes now

items.question: Should I buy a used, reconditioned, or rebuilt Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engines offer the strongest long-term value with upgraded components, bench testing, and 12-month warranty coverage. Used units are cheapest but carry unknown history; reconditioned sit in the middle with limited upgrades.
keyPoints: Used DT306: Lowest upfront cost but no warranty beyond supplier guarantee
Reconditioned: Partial strip, cleaned parts, basic warranty 3-6 months typical
Rebuilt: Full strip, upgraded wear components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
items.warning: Never fit a used DT306 without compression and leak-down testing - hidden wear can cause rapid repeat failure.
items.cta: -> Get tailored quotes for rebuilt Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engines today

items.question: How long should a Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engines can reliably reach 150,000-200,000 miles with strict adherence to service intervals and quality oil changes. Short-trip urban driving without regular motorway runs may reduce lifespan due to DPF regeneration demands.
keyPoints: Follow 12-month or 12,000-mile service intervals maximum for DT306 longevity
Use only JLR-approved low-viscosity oil to protect turbo and timing components
Regular motorway runs help complete DPF regeneration cycles and reduce oil dilution risk
Address any warning lights immediately to prevent minor issues escalating
Check Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 service specialists in your area
items.cta: -> Check Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 service specialists in your area

items.question: Does the Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engine have any known recalls or technical service bulletins?
items.answer: Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engines have had recalls for oil filter housing cracks and water pump pulley drive belt issues, both addressable at authorised dealers at no cost to owners under warranty.
keyPoints: Oil filter housing crack recall affects some 2020-2022 DT306 builds - check VIN with dealer
Water pump pulley drive belt recall prevents premature belt failure and accessory damage
Always verify recall status via Land Rover's official recall checker before purchasing used
Independent specialists can perform recall-related repairs if dealer access is limited
Verify your Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 recall status before engine work
items.cta: -> Verify your Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 recall status before engine work

items.question: Can a Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engine be repaired instead of replaced?
items.answer: Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engines can sometimes be repaired for isolated issues like turbo replacement or EGR cleaning, but catastrophic internal damage usually makes full replacement more cost-effective and reliable long-term.
keyPoints: Turbo replacement: £1,800-£3,500 at specialist, viable if block and internals are sound
EGR or DPF-related repairs: £800-£2,200 depending on component access and labour
Internal damage (bearing wear, piston scoring): replacement almost always better value
Always get a full diagnostic report before committing to major repair spend
items.warning: Attempting minor repairs on a DT306 with spun bearings or scored cylinders risks total engine seizure during road testing.
items.cta: -> Get a free diagnostic assessment for your Land Rover Defender 90 D250 DT306 engine
